title: piao L1 Event
description: piao-pipeline L1 kernel event per kernel/03-layered-architecture.md §3.1 + §4. Events are written to append-only JSONL journals at $PL_OUTPUT/piao/kernel-events/<YYYY-MM>.jsonl. Immutability guaranteed by §4.1 bottom line 1.

event_id.description: Event identity. Format: <domain>-<YYMMDDHHmmss>-<6-char suffix>. Suffix is normally 6 hex chars for new events; historical events (pre-M7 prefix taxonomy decision) may use other 3-10 char identifiers such as 'rpt001'. See kernel/01-identity-model.md §5 and kernel/03-layered-architecture.md §4.1.

event_type.description: L1 event type from the locked 10-class enum per §3.1. Extensions require kernel spec rev bump.

event_layer.description: Event layer. L1 = kernel (this schema); L2 = adapter-defined business events (see §5).

emitted_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime with timezone. UTC preferred for deterministic ordering.

emitted_by.description: Actor URN that emitted this event. Typically an agent or manual tool URN.

subject.description: The URN this event is about. See kernel/01-identity-model.md §5.3 subject+semantic-field dual-track rule.

producer_task_urn.description: Task URN that produced the subject artifact. Applies to artifact.* events.

artifact_urn.description: Semantic field for artifact.* events; must equal subject when present (§5.3).

content_sha256.description: Artifact content sha256 (lowercase hex). Optional for historical events; required for all new artifact.published events per M7 §2 three-tuple (URN + rev + sha256) contract.

artifact_kind.description: Short alias of subject's kind segment. Convenience field for filtering, not authoritative. See urn.schema.json#/$defs/kind.

artifact_type.description: Finer-grained type derived from front-matter kind: per kernel/02-artifact-model.md §2.2.4 type registration.

published_at.description: When the subject artifact was logically published. Often equal to emitted_at but can differ for reconstructed events (§5.3.1 historical compatibility).

reconstructed.description: True iff this event is a back-fill for a historical artifact. See §5.3.1.

reconstructed_from.description: Free-text provenance reason for the back-fill.

supersedes.description: URN of the artifact this one replaces (applicable to artifact.published / artifact.superseded).

resolves.description: Debt-ledger item IDs this event resolves (e.g. ["Q29"]).

drift_artifact_urn.description: drift.detected specific: the drift artifact URN.

drift_artifact_sha256.description: drift.detected specific: sha256 of the drift artifact body.

drift_kind.description: drift.detected specific: drift classification per kernel/05-drift-propagation.md §3.5.

scope_kind.description: drift.detected specific: the scope kind matching source snapshots.

drift_event_urn.description: evolution.scanned specific: URN of the drift.detected event being scanned.

decision.description: evolution.scanned specific: 4-A pure-observation is always pass_through per m6-final-decisions §1 R4/R5.

evidence.description: Optional evidence object carrying provenance details (sha / event_id / ...).

content_sha256_note.description: Human-readable note about the sha value (e.g. historical_untraceable, final rev byte-traceable).

event_id_prefix_note.description: Annotation when event_id prefix reflects M7 taxonomy decisions (Q29 B'-1).
